rulings.Case Title: SAMETC, LLC et al VS. TED AND PAULINE ELMS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, line 1. Motion For New Trial DENIED on all bases as listed in the Notice of Intention to Move for New Trial and Memorandum of Points and Authorities. Sufficient evidence was presented to the court regarding jurisdiction and binding effect of the arbitrator's decisions as to all TIC members/parties to the Four-Way Agreement. The Subdivided Lands Act issue was improperly raised for the first time at the hearing. Appeal in case 501631 has no bearing on the petition at bar. Additionally, even though respondents did not comply with the requirements of CCP 1291 and 632 in properly requesting a statement of decision ("The request for a statement of decision shall specify those controverted issues as to which the party is requesting a statement of decision."), the Court explained "the factual and legal basis for its decision" in the September 17, 2014 Amended Order re: Petition to Confirm Arbitration. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TESHOME T TAREKEGN, VS. FREWWORK ABERRA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Reconsider And For Leave To Amend Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, line 2. PLAINTIFF TESHOME TAREKEGN's Motion To Reconsider And For Leave To Amend Second Amended Complaint DENIED. Plaintiff fails to sustain burden under CCP 1008(b). Additionally, even if the Court construes this motion as a CCP 473(b) motion, it is also DENIED as no proposed pleading was submitted with the motion. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MCCORMACK BARON RAGAN MGMT. SERVICES, INC. VS. TERRY MATHEWS et al

rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, line 7. DEFENDANT TERRY MATHEWS' Petition For Relief From Forfeiture, CCP 1179 DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden under CCP 1179. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIANO BUSTO et al VS. ALEJANDRO V. DIESTA JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer-Sets A, B And C From Pltffs And Each Of Them; Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT ALEX DIESTA, JR's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer-Sets A, B And C From Plaintiffs; and for Sanctions GRANTED in part and DENIED. Motion is denied as to requests regarding information about other tenants' evictions; the construction project to renovate the building; Plaintiffs' income and assets; and financial arrangements between Plaintiffs regarding joint ownership. Motion is otherwise GRANTED. Parties are ordered to appear at 9:00 a.m. and engage in meet and confer efforts to outline the specific requests to which motion is granted/denied in accordance with the tentative ruling. No sanctions. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIANO BUSTO et al VS. ALEJANDRO V. DIESTA JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Further Responses To His Demand For Production, Inspection And Copying Of Documents-Set As From Pltffs, And Each Of Them; Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT ALEX DIESTA, JR's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To His Demand For Production, Inspection And Copying Of Documents-Set As From Plaintiffs, and for Sanctions GRANTED in part and DENIED. Motion is denied as to requests regarding information about other tenants' evictions, the construction project to renovate the building, Plaintiffs' income and assets and financial arrangements between Plaintiffs regarding joint ownership. Motion is otherwise GRANTED. Parties are ordered to appear at 9:00 a.m. and engage in meet and confer efforts to outline the specific requests to which motion is granted/denied in accordance with the tentative ruling. No sanctions. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MEISLIN VS. ARKADIY ZLOBINSKIY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT ARKADIY ZLOBINSKIY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to (1) attach a complete copy of the lease or fill out paragraph 6(f) of the form complaint; and (2) to address any and all relevant changes to the agreement in paragraph 6(d) of the form complaint. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. SHEILA WILLIAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 13, 2014, line 10. DEFENDANT SHEILA WILLIAMS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective. No opposition filed. = (501/REQ)
